description: Accenture is a leading global professional services company, providing a broad range of services and solutions in strategy, consulting, digital, technology and operations. With 482,000 people serving clients in more than 120 countries, Accenture drives innovation to improve the way the world works and lives. Accenture has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2023.

Adobe is a multinational software company based in the US. The company is committed to powering its operations and the digital delivery of its products entirely with renewable electricity by 2035 as one of five ambitious goals that will contribute to a low-carbon, sustainable future.

                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              

  
    Learn more

AESC is headquartered in Yokohama, Japan and was founded in 2007. Developing and manufacturing batteries for zero-emission electric vehicles and energy storage systems, AESC has operations in the U.S., U.K., Europe, Japan and China with over 12,000 employees. AESC has set a target to secure all of its electricity from renewable sources which is compliant with the RE100 technical criteria by 2025.

Airbnb was born in 2008 when two Hosts welcomed three guests to their San Francisco home, and has since grown to 4 million Hosts who have welcomed over 800 million guest arrivals in almost every country across the globe. Airbnb is committed to using 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2021, and is working with their community of Hosts to encourage the adoption of renewable electricity for their homes.

Anheuser-Busch InBev is the world’s largest brewer, based in Leuven, Belgium. Its diverse portfolio of well over 400 beer brands leverages the collective strengths of 200,000 employees in 50 countries. AB InBev has committed to transitioning its global operations to 100% renewable electricity and has set a target to secure all of its purchased electricity from renewable sources by 2025.

                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              

  
    Learn more

Apple lead the world in innovation with iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch and Apple TV. Apple works to power its global facilities across 43 countries with renewable electricity. Apple is also helping its manufacturing partners to lower its carbon footprint, working with them to install more than 4 gigawatts of new clean energy worldwide by 2020.

AstraZeneca is a global, science-led biopharmaceutical business that focuses on the discovery, development and commercialisation of prescription medicines. The company is committed to doing its 'fair share' to protect the planet and to keeping employees safe and healthy. AstraZeneca has a goal to source 100% renewable electricity globally by 2025, with an interim target of 100% in Europe and the US by 2020.

With over 325 years of history and expertise in banking, Barclays operates in over 40 countries and employs approximately 83,500 people. Barclays moves, lends, invests and protects money for customers and clients worldwide. Barclays has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity by 2030 and 90% by 2025.

Burberry is a global luxury fashion brand with a distinctive British identity, founded in 1856. As a global retailer and manufacturer, Burberry employs over 10,000 people and have over 400 retail locations worldwide. Burberry is aiming to procure 100% of electricity from renewable resources to power its whole business by 2022.

The Carlsberg Group, headquartered in Denmark and one of the world’s biggest brewers, is switching to 100% renewable electricity at its breweries by 2022, as a step towards its target to become carbon neutral in 2030.

CHANEL is a private company and a world leader in creating, developing, manufacturing and distributing luxury products. In 2019, CHANEL achieved 41% renewable electricity for its owned and operated facilities globally. In 2020, the company joined RE100 with a commitment to achieve 100% renewable electricity by 2025, with an interim target of 97% by 2021.

Chindata Group is a leading carrier-neutral hyperscale data centre solution provider in the Asian-Pacific emerging markets. It is a first mover in developing next-generation hyperscale data centers in China, India and Southeast Asia. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.
Danone is a leading global food company targeting 100% renewable electricity by 2030, with an interim goal of 50% by 2020.

Decathlon is a leading sports retailer in Europe and around the globe, with presence in over 50 countries. Decathlon joined RE100 with a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity for its global operations by 2026.

Deloitte is a leading global provider of audit and assurance, consulting, financial advisory, risk advisory, tax, and related services. Deloitte employs approximately 345,000 people in more than 150 countries and territories worldwide. Deloitte has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2030.

Delta is a global provider of power and thermal management solutions headquartered in Taiwan. As an energy-saving solutions provider with core competencies in power electronics and automation, Delta's business categories include Power Electronics, Automation, and Infrastructure. The company has a target to source 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

DuPont is a global specialty products company empowering the world with the essential innovations to thrive. DuPont commits to sourcing 60% renewable electricity by 2030, transitioning all global operations to 100% renewable electricity by 2050.

Envision, a global leading wind turbine technology company, wishes to create a world where everyone has access to clean, secure and affordable energy. Headquartered in Shanghai, Envision has regional offices across Asia, Europe, North and South Americas. Envision is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2025.

EY exists to build a better, more sustainable working world – creating long-term value for clients, people and society, and building trust in the capital markets. Enabled by data and technology, diverse EY teams in over 150 countries provide trust through assurance and help clients grow, transform and operate. EY is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2025.

First Solar’s advanced photovoltaic (PV) solar module technology delivers the lowest carbon solar energy available today. It is a leading provider of comprehensive PV solar solutions with more than 25-gigawatts of modules shipped to over 45 countries worldwide. From raw material sourcing through end-of-life module recycling, its renewable energy solutions protect and enhance the environment. First Solar has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2028, with an interim goal of transitioning its facilities in the United States, where it is headquartered and operates the Western Hemisphere’s largest solar manufacturing footprint, to carbon-free electricity by 2026.

Fujitsu is the leading Japanese information and communication technology (ICT) company, offering a full range of technology products, solutions, and services. Approximately 140,000 Fujitsu people support customers in more than 100 countries. Fujitsu has set a target to source 100% renewable electricity by 2030.

General Motors is a global automobile manufacturer that plans to meet the electricity needs of all its global facilities with 100% renewable sources by 2035. GM also plans to meet its US demand with renewable electricity by 2025. This goal, along with the pursuit of electrified vehicles and responsible manufacturing, is part of the company’s approach to strengthening its business, improving communities and addressing climate change.

Goldwind is a global leader of clean energy, energy conservation, and environmental protection, headquartered in China. Specialising in wind power, the company leverages strong scientific research and innovation to take renewable energy utilisation and efficiency to new heights. Goldwind commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2031.

Google is a global technology leader focused on improving the ways people connect with information. Google’s innovations in web search and advertising have made its website a top Internet property and its brand one of the most recognised in the world.

Grupo Bimbo is the global leader in the baking industry and is headquartered in Mexico. The company will power its operations with 100% renewable electricity by 2025, with an interim target of 80% by 2020.
Learn more about Grupo Bimbo's Activities.

Hyundai Motor Company, is a global automobile manufacturing company headquartered in Seoul, Republic of Korea. Hyundai cars are sold in over 190 countries worldwide, and they are planning to support renewable technology in all areas of their business, expanding their electric and hydrogen powered vehicles. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations and any majority owned subsidiaries by 2045. It joins the initiative along with Hyundai Transys, Hyundai KEFICO, Hyundai Partecs, Hyundai Capital, Hyundai NGV, MOCEAN.

The Inditex Group is a family of several commercial brands: Zara, Pull&Bear, Massimo Dutti, Bershka, Stradivarius, Oysho and Zara Home. Its headquarters are located in Arteixo, Spain and its brands operate across over 200 markets worldwide. The group set a target to procure 100% renewable electricity in 2022 which it announced achieving. RE100 is able to recognise that 96% of the Group’s claims were in accordance with the RE100 technical criteria, not reaching 100% mostly due to limitations in certain geographies. The technical criteria aims to emphasise the impact of corporate renewables procurement and was followed by Inditex wherever possible. Inditex joins RE100 with the goal of transitioning the final 4% of its renewables procurement to become successfully validated as per the RE100 technical requirements by 2025.

Ingka Group (formerly IKEA Group) operates three business areas: IKEA Retail, Ingka Investments and Ingka Centres. Ingka Group operates 367 IKEA stores in 30 countries, as well as shopping centres and increasingly city-centre customer meeting points. The company has committed to generate as much renewable energy as the total energy it consumes in its operations by 2020; as well as to consume 100% renewable electricity by 2025. Alongside Swiss Re, Ingka Group is a founding partner of the RE100 initiative.

                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              

  
    Learn more

JinkoSolar is a global leader in the solar industry. The company distributes its solar products and sells its solutions and services to a diversified international utility, commercial and residential customer base in across the world. JinkoSolar committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030 with an interim target of 70% by 2023.

Johnson & Johnson is the world’s largest and most broadly-based health care company, and its mission is to help people live longer, healthier and happier lives. Recognising the intrinsic link between a healthy environment and human health, the company wants to play its part in addressing climate change and has set a target to power all of its facilities with renewable electricity by 2025.

Kering is a global luxury group owning several renowned houses in fashion, leather goods, jewellery and watches. These houses include Gucci, Saint Laurent, Bottega Veneta, Balenciaga, Alexander McQueen, Brioni, Boucheron, Pomellato, DoDo, Qeelin, Ulysse Nardin, Girard-Perregaux, and Kering Eyewear. Kering has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2022.

Kia Corporation is a global mobility brand with a vision to create sustainable mobility solutions for consumers, communities, and societies around the world. Founded in 1944, Kia has been providing mobility solutions for more than 75 years. With 52,000 employees worldwide, a presence in more than 190 markets, and manufacturing facilities in six countries, the company today sells around three million vehicles a year. As part of Kia's carbon neutrality strategy, the company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040, with an interim target of 60% by 2030.

Kingwhale Corporation is a leading producer of performance fabric and garments for world-renowned brands. Headquartered in Taiwan, and with multiple overseas operations, Kingwhale ensures the production and lifecycle of its products have the lowest possible impact on the environment. Kingwhale, the first textile manufacturer in Asia-Pacific to join RE100, commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2040.

Korea Zinc is a global company specialized in refining nonferrous metals. It produces about 1.1 million tons of total 14 types of base and precious metals from zinc to lead, gold, silver, and copper. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

KT Group is a digital platform group that operates in AI, BigData, and Cloud. The group provides advanced information and communications technology (ICT) services based on convergence between cutting-edge IoT technology and business sectors. KT Group has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

LG Energy Solution is one of the world’s largest lithium-ion battery manufacturers with a market-leading position in advanced batteries for EV, energy storage system (ESS) and Mobility & IT applications. Its commitment to technology leadership coupled with efficient and high-quality manufacturing processes produces batteries that exhibit the highest levels of safety, performance and reliability. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

LG Innotek develops and manufactures high-tech materials and components used by mobile, display, semiconductor, car, and IoT products in the business areas of Optics Solution, Substrate & Material, Automotive Components and Electronic Components. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

LONGi is the world’s most valuable solar technology company, developing product innovations and breakthrough technologies. It supplies about a quarter of global market demand for high-efficiency solar wafers and modules. Innovation and sustainable development are two of LONGi’s core values. LONGi has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2028 with an interim target of 70% by 2027.

LOTTE Chemical, headquartered in South Korea, was founded in 1976 and is a global general chemical company, specialising in providing chemical products and chemical material solution services. The company is continuously looks to protect nature and make human life more abundant through the development of products using eco-friendly technologies. As part of LOTTE Chemical's 'Net Zero' strategy, the company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

Macquarie Group Limited is a global diversified financial organisation. Headquartered in Australia, Macquarie employs ~15,700 people in 31 markets. Macquarie has committed to powering its global operations with 100% renewable electricity by 2025.

Meta's mission is to give people the power to share and make the world more open and connected. The company is committed to powering connectivity with the smallest footprint possible, and set a goal of reaching 50% clean and renewable energy in its electricity supply mix for its data centers in 2018. Meta has a goal to run its entire operations on 100% renewable electricity by 2020.

Microsoft, based in the US, is the leading platform and productivity company for the mobile-first, cloud-first world, and its mission is to empower every person and organisation on the planet to achieve more.

Nexans provides an extensive range of cables and cabling solutions that transmit power and information to millions of people, communities and industries worldwide. The company has a target to source 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

NIKE, Inc. is the world's leading designer, marketer and distributor of authentic athletic footwear, apparel, equipment and accessories for a wide variety of sports and fitness activities. NIKE, Inc. is committed to reaching 100% renewable electricity by 2025.

Nissin Foods Holdings is a Japanese food company that produces instant noodles and convenience food. With numerous food brands, their products are sold in more than 100 countries worldwide. Nissin Foods Holdings has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.

Novartis is a leading global medicines company that uses innovative science and digital technologies to create transformative treatments in areas of great medical need. Novartis has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2025.

PwC is a network of professional service firms operating in over 150 countries around the world. PwC joined RE100 with a commitment to source 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030. Transitioning its largest firms first, PwC has an interim goal to power 70% of its operations with renewable electricity by 2022.

Samsung Biologics is a South Korean biotechnology company providing offerings from development, manufacturing, to bioanalytical testing services at every stage for biopharmaceutical products while meeting the evolving needs of the global healthcare industry. It joins the initiative alongside its subsidiary Samsung Bioepis, a biopharmaceutical company focused on increasing patient access to high-quality medicines through the development of biologic medicines. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

Samsung Electro-Mechanics is a global multi-component manufacturing company that develops and produces advanced electronic and mechanical components. Headquartered in South Korea, the company is a B2B company centred on Component, Optics & Communication Solution, and Package Solution. Samsung Electro-Mechanics commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2050.

Samsung Electronics, headquartered in the Republic of Korea, has been at the forefront of the global electronics market, shaping the future with transformative ideas and technologies. The company is redefining the worlds of TVs, smartphones, wearable devices, tablets, digital appliances, network systems, memory, system LSI, foundry and LED solutions. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.

Schneider Electric is a leader in the digital transformation of energy management and automation. The French company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030, 80% by 2020.
Learn more about Schneider's activities.

Signify is the global leader in connected LED products, systems and services. As the largest lighting company in the world, it offers solutions across the value chain and is uniquely positioned to bring more and better light to the world and help provide a more sustainable future. Signify has made a commitment to powering 100% of its operations with renewable electricity by 2020.

Starbucks is a global coffee company committed to ethically sourcing and roasting the highest quality Arabica coffee. As part of RE100, Starbucks is committed to 100% renewable electricity and is exploring ways to further increase its use of renewable electricity as part of its global energy mix.

Sun Metals Corporation Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Korea Zinc which is the largest base metal and precious metal producer, manufactures special high grade zinc using its own integrated industrial solar plant in Australia. Korea Zinc Group is considered to have the most advanced technologies of metals refining in the world. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2040.

Sungrow is a global leading inverter solution supplier. The company has a broad product portfolio offering PV inverter solutions and energy storage systems for utility-scale, commercial, industrial, and residential applications, internationally recognized floating PV plant solutions as well as wind energy converters. With a 23-year track record in the PV space, Sungrow products power installations in over 60 countries. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2028.
Learn more about Sungrow's activities.

T-Mobile US, Inc. is an American wireless network operator delivering services to over 70 million people, redefining the way that consumers and businesses buy wireless services through leading product and service innovation. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2021.
Tetra Pak is a Swedish food processing and packaging company committed to powering its operations with 100% renewable electricity by 2030, with an interim goal to reach 80% by 2020.

Tongwei Co., Ltd. is a large-scale private technology-based listed company with the dual main businesses of agriculture and new energy as its core. It is headquartered in Chengdu, China. The company now has more than 200 branches and subsidiaries all over the country and overseas, with more than 60,000 employees. Tongwei Co., Ltd commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2030.

Switzerland-based UBS is a leading global financial services provider with a long track record of addressing environmental and energy challenges, determined to support its clients in preparing for success in an increasingly carbon-constricted world. The company has a goal to be powered by 100% renewable electricity by 2020, to reduce its greenhouse gas footprint by 75% by compared with 2004 levels.

Unilever is a British-Dutch multinational consumer goods company that believes sustainability and emission reduction must be achieved in tandem with its brand growth. Having already switched to entirely renewable electricity in Europe and the US, the company has an interim target to source all its electricity purchased from the grid from renewables by 2020, with a view to sourcing 100% of its energy from renewables by 2030.

Vail Resorts is a leading global mountain resort operator, with 11 world-class mountain resorts and three urban ski areas in the US, Canada and Australia, and a collection of casually elegant hotels. The environment is central to Vail Resort’s business, and so the company feels a special obligation to protect it. Vail Resorts aims to source 100% renewable electricity by 2030, helping to deliver on its ‘Epic Promise for a Zero Footprint’ by the same year.

Founded in 1994, Vanguard International Semiconductor Corporation (VIS) is a leading specialty IC foundry service provider headquartered in Taiwan. VIS has five 8-inch fabs in Taiwan and Singapore with a monthly capacity of about 262,000 wafers and a total of over 6,000 employees in 2022. VIS has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Westpac is an Australian bank which provides a broad range of consumer, business and institutional banking and wealth management services. The company has a target to source 100% renewable electricity by 2025, with an interim target of 45% by 2021.

Zurich Insurance Group (Zurich) is a leading multi-line insurer, providing customers in global and local markets with a wide range of property, casualty, and life insurance products and services in more than 210 countries and territories. Zurich has a target to use 100% renewable electricity globally by the end of 2022.

ABB is a leading global technology company, based in Switzerland, aiming to achieve a more productive and sustainable future across its electrification, robotics, automation and motion portfolio. ABB has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

Acer is one of the world's top ICT companies with a presence in more than 160 countries. As the company evolves with industry and changing lifestyles, it is focused on enabling a world where hardware, software and services fuse to create ecosystems, allowing new possibilities for consumers and businesses. Acer is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2035 and is actively engaging with its supply chain to promote the uptake of renewables, to maximise the impact of its commitment.

Advantech, headquartered in Taiwan, is a leader in IoT intelligent systems and embedded platforms with several business groups including: Industrial IoT Group, the Embedded IoT Group, the Service IoT Group, and the Industrial Cloud and Video Group. Advantech promotes IoT solutions with the Edge Intelligence WISE-PaaS core to assist connecting industrial chains. Advantech is also working with partners to co-create ecosystems that accelerate industrial intelligence, with a vision to enable an intelligent planet. Advantech is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Advantest Corporation is a leading manufacturer of equipment for the semiconductor industry. Based in Tokyo, it also produces instruments used in the design, production and maintenance of electronic systems globally. Advantest Corporation commits to source 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

AEO Inc. is a global retailer offering on-trend clothing, accessories and personal care products under its American Eagle and Aerie brands. They operate more than 1,000 stores and ships to 81 countries worldwide. AEO commits to securing renewable energy for 100% of electrical power demand for owned and operated facilities by 2030.

AEON Co., Ltd is a global retailer operating more than 600 general merchandise stores and 303 shopping malls in Japan and overseas. AEON joined RE100 with a target of sourcing 100% renewable electricity to power its global business operations by 2050.
AirTrunk is an Asia Pacific & Japan data centre operator, headquartered in Sydney. AirTrunk commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

The Ajinomoto Group aims to provide food and health-related solutions that improve dietary habits and support aging populations, and create greater wellness for people all around the world. The company has offices in 35 countries and regions and sells products in more than 130 countries and regions. Ajinomoto Group commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity to power its global business operations by 2050.
AkzoNobel is a Dutch paints and coatings company aiming to be carbon neutral and use 100% renewable energy – heat as well as electricity – by 2030.

The Allianz Group is one of the world's leading insurers and asset managers with more than 88 million retail and corporate customers. As one of the world’s largest investors, Allianz is managing over €660 billion on behalf of its insurance customers while its asset managers Allianz Global Investors and PIMCO manage more than €1.4 trillion of third-party assets. Allianz joined RE100 with a target to source 100% renewable electricity across their global operations by 2023.

Alps Alpine is a Japanese multinational corporation, headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, producing electronic devices, including switches, potentiometers, sensors, encoders and touchpads. The company’s aim is to transform into a sustainable value-creating corporate group, with a focus on three business segments —Components, Sensors & Communication and Modules & Systems. Alps Alpine commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

Alstria is one of the largest real estate companies in Germany. Having moved towards 100% fossil-free electricity in 2013, the company has a goal to roll out 100% renewable electricity across its entire portfolio by 2020.

AMADA Co Ltd. is a large Japanese manufacturer of metal processing equipment and machinery based in Kanagawa. The AMADA Group consists of 90 subsidiaries and affiliated companies, headed by AMADA Co., Ltd, whose main businesses are the manufacture, sale, lease, repair, maintenance, and inspection of metalworking machines and equipment. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Amalgamated Bank, as part of its commitment to values-based banking, joined RE100 to call on the rest of the industry to follow its lead and take significant steps to proactively address climate risk and reduce carbon pollution. Amalgamated Bank achieved its goal to source 100% renewable electricity in 2017.

American Express is a globally integrated payments company, providing customers with access to products, insights and experiences that enrich lives and build business success. American Express set a goal to remain carbon neutral and powered by 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2018. Since then, American Express used onsite solar and procured renewable electricity to match its entire global electricity demand across its operations, 98% of which is compliant with the RE100 technical criteria.

Amorepacific is a South Korean beauty company operating over 20 beauty, personal care, and health care brands.  The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2025.

Ansell is a diversified global leader in protection solutions – integrated manufacturer, innovator and marketer of products on which millions of workers and healthcare professionals rely on. Operating in two business segments, Industrial and Healthcare, Ansell employees over 14,000 people worldwide with manufacturing operations in Latin America, EMEA, and Asia Pacific and customers in over 100 countries around the world trust Ansell and its products. Ansell’s ambitious environmental goals strive towards provision of sustainable protection. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2040, with an interim target of 60% by 2030.

ANZ is an Australian multinational banking and financial services company headquartered in Melbourne, serving more than 5 million customers and operating in its primary markets of Australia and New Zealand, as well as Asia, the Pacific, UK/Europe and the US. ANZ has committed to powering 100% of its global operations with renewable electricity by 2025.

Applied Materials, Inc. (Nasdaq: AMAT) is the leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. Headquartered in the Silicon Valley, their expertise in modifying materials at atomic levels and on an industrial scale enables customers to transform possibilities into reality. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

Ardentec Corporation is a multinational semiconductor testing service provider headquartered in Taiwan and have established a global presence through long-term cooperative relationship with big domestic and foreign semiconductor manufacturers. Since its establishment in 1999, they have become one of the top three specialized semiconductor testing service providers in Taiwan through industry-leading engineering capability, quality systems and IT technical service, and industrial vertical integration. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2050.

Arm is a semiconductor and software design company whose advanced intellectual property products have shipped in more than 180 billion chips, powering everything from tiny sensors to the world’s most powerful supercomputer. Arm has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2023.

Arrow Electronics is an American Fortune 500 company, founded in 1935 and headquartered in Centennial, Colorado. With over 22,000 employees globally, Arrow is a global provider of technology products and services, specializing in electronic components and enterprise computing products. Arrow joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations sites by 2030.
Asahi Group Holdings, Ltd. Is a Japanese global beer, wine, spirits, soft drinks and food business group. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity for its global operations by 2040.

Asahi Kasei Homes Corp. is a leading housing manufacturer in Japan, providing long-life detached houses and apartment housings (“HEBEL HAUS”) that take the environment and energy into consideration throughout the life cycle of housing. In order to promote the use of renewable energy and realise a decarbonised society, Asahi Kasei Homes aims to procure 100% of its electricity from renewable energy by 2023.

Ashok Leyland, a US $ 4.5 billion company, is the 2nd largest manufacturer of commercial vehicles in India, and the 4th largest manufacturer of buses in the world.  It’s diverse product portfolio covers trucks, buses, power solutions which include alternate fuels (CNG, LNG, Hydrogen) and BEV options. It is a forerunner in introducing cutting-edge technologies demonstrating energy conservation and industry leading renewable energy initiatives in manufacturing process as well as its products. Ashok Leyland commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2030.

ASICS is a Japanese company which produces sports goods such as footwear, apparel and equipment designed for a range of sports. ASICS has set a target of sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

ASKUL Corporation is a Japanese e-commerce company offering a wide variety of products to households and companies across Japan. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2030, with an interim goal of 80% by 2025.

ASM is a Dutch headquartered multinational corporation that specializes in the design, manufacturing, sales and service of semiconductor wafer processing equipment needed to produce semiconductor devices, or integrated circuits (ICs). With a track record of more than 50 years of innovation, ASM has always been ahead of what’s next. Continually delivering new technology and product innovations, the company helps the leading semiconductor manufacturers create the advanced chips of the future - providing the increased power needed for next-generation computers, smartphones, home electronics and transport. ASM has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2024.

ASOS, founded in 2000 and headquartered in London, United Kingdom, operates as a global fashion retailer. With 20 million active customers across over 200 markets, ASOS employs around 3,000 people. The company offers a wide range of fashion products, primarily catering to its 20-something customer base. ASOS commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2027.

Asset Management One Co., Ltd. is one of the largest asset managers in Asia, providing a broad and diverse range of investment solutions to institutional and retail clients. Asset Management One has set a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity by 2050 for a sustainable future.

ASUS is a leading multinational electronics company headquartered in Taiwan. it is known for motherboards, PCs, monitors, graphics cards and routers. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2035.

Atlassian is an Australian enterprise software company that develops products for improving software development, project management, collaboration, and code quality. Atlassian achieved its goal to source 100% renewable electricity in 2020, five years ahead of schedule.

AUO Corporation specialises in optoelectronic solutions. It offers a full range of display applications and integrated solutions by leveraging core expertise to enter vertical markets including smart retail, smart transportation, general health, solar energy, circular economy and smart manufacturing service. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.
Aurora Organic Dairy is a leading producer and processor of high-quality organic milk and butter for retail store brands. The company has a goal to source 100% renewable electricity by 2020.
Autodesk is an American multinational software corporation that achieved its goal to source 100% renewable power in 2016 – four years ahead of schedule.

Avient Corporation (NYSE: AVNT) is a global provider of specialized and sustainable material solutions. The company is certified ACC Responsible Care®, a founding member of the Alliance to End Plastic Waste and certified Great Place to Work®. It commits to source 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

Aviva is a British multinational insurance company providing savings, retirement, insurance, health and asset management products and services. The company is working to increase the amount of renewable electricity they purchase for its operations globally and have a goal to procure 100% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2025.

AXA is a French insurance company targeting 100% renewable electricity by 2025. Operating in more than 60 countries with diverse energy markets, AXA intends to achieve this target by using a mix of approaches, notably buying electricity directly from providers and compensating for non-renewable electricity.

Bagmane Group is a commercial real estate developer, established in 1996 and headquartered in Bengaluru, India. With over 30 million square feet of operational space across marquee projects like Bagmane Tech Park, Bagmane World Technology Centre, Bagmane Constellation Business Park, Bagmane Capital and Solarium City, the Group is redefining how businesses interact with the environment. Bagmane joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2030.

Bank Australia is a customer-owned bank that believes in the importance of using their customers’ money in responsible ways. Bank Australia announced it had achieved their 100% target a year early, in 2019.

Gürmen Group operates within the fields of retail, mining, energy, agriculture, construction and real estate. The group, situated in Turkey, employs around 3,000 people and exports its products to 60 countries worldwide. The company is already powered by 100% renewable electricity.
H&M, the popular Swedish fashion retailer, is working to achieve 100% renewable power by 2030 – and is already nearly there.

Hamamatsu Photonics K.K. is a Japanese manufacturer of optical sensors, electric light sources, and other optical devices and their applied instruments for scientific, technical and medical use. The company manufactures and sells Photomultiplier Tubes, Imaging Devices, Light Sources, Opto-Semiconductors, Imaging and Analyzing Systems. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Hang Lung Properties Limited is headquartered in China, Hong Kong SAR and develops and manages a portfolio of properties in both China, Hong Kong SAR and nine Mainland Chinese cities: Shanghai, Shenyang, Jinan, Wuxi, Tianjin, Dalian, Kunming, Wuhan, and Hangzhou. The company’s mixed-use developments in these Mainland cities operate under the '66' brand. The company joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

HARMAN designs and engineers connected products and solutions for automakers, consumers, and enterprises worldwide. HARMAN strives to enhance the social and environmental fabric of the communities and markets it serves through sustainable innovation and rich experiences. HARMAN is committed to achieving 100% renewable electricity across our global footprint by 2030.

Hazama Ando Corporation, headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, is a large construction company with overseas operations across Asia as well as the US, Mexico, Central and South America. The company has a target to use 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.

HD Hyundai Xitesolution is a holding company representing HD Hyundai’s Construction Machinery sector, HD Construction Equipment and HD Hyundai Infracore. The company and it’s construction machinery business has an established product portfolio encompassing excavators and loaders, special equipment, and industrial vehicles. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2040.

Heathrow Airport is the UK’s only hub airport, serving over 1,300 flights and 200,000 passengers every day. Heathrow 2.0 is the airport’s plan for sustainable growth, including a goal to become a zero-carbon airport by 2050 for fixed infrastructure. To help deliver on this, Heathrow has sourced 100% renewable electricity since April 2017, with an increasing proportion coming from on-site generation.

HEINEKEN is an international brewer and a leading developer and marketer of premium beer and cider brands. Led by the Heineken® brand, the Group has a portfolio of more than 300 international, regional, local and specialty beers and ciders. It employs over 84,000 employees and operates breweries, malteries, cider plants and other production facilities in more than 70 countries. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

Helvetia is a Swiss-based international insurance group, serving more than 7 million customers. The company is committed to continuously reducing its overall carbon footprint. Helvetia has sourced 100% renewable electricity since 2015 and became carbon neutral in 2017.

Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is an industry-leading technology company headquartered in Palo Alto, California. HPE Technology and services aim to help customers worldwide make IT more efficient, productive and secure. Committed to delivering a better future, HPE has set an interim goal to source 50% of its total electricity consumption from renewable sources by 2025, with a long-term intent of reaching 100% renewable electricity in future.

HNI Corporation and its family of global brands offer leading workplace furnishings and residential building products. The company has set a target to source 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

The Home Depot is the world's largest home improvement specialty retailer. The company operates a total of 2,298 retail stores in all 50 states, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, U.S. Virgin Islands, Guam, 10 Canadian provinces and Mexico. The company commits to sourcing 100 percent renewable electricity for its entire operations by 2030, further extending its efforts to improve the environment through cleaner energy.

Hon Hai Technology Group (Foxconn), based in Taiwan, is the world’s largest electronics manufacturer and a technological solution provider, integrating its manufacturing systems with emerging technologies. The Group is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2040.

HOYA Corporation, founded in 1941 in Tokyo, is a global med-tech company and a leading supplier of innovative high-tech and medical products. HOYA has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2040, with an interim target of 60% by 2030.
HP Inc., a global leader in printing and personal systems, has made a commitment to use 100% renewable energy to power its electricity use globally by 2025.

HSBC is one of the world's largest banking and financial services organisations, serving around 38 million customers through four global businesses: Retail Banking and Wealth Management, Commercial Banking, Global Banking and Markets, and Global Private Banking. HSBC's network covers 67 countries and territories in Europe, Asia, the Middle East and Africa, North America and Latin America. The bank is targeting 100% renewable electricity by 2030, with an interim goal of 90% by 2025.

Hulic Co., Ltd. is a Japanese real estate company centred on the real estate leasing and investment business in central Tokyo. The company has committed to transitioning its operations to 100% renewable electricity by 2024.

Hyundai Mobis, based in the Republic of Korea, is a global auto parts manufacturer with leading expertise in automotive system solution, module manufacturing, and after-sales parts. Moving forward, the company pursues continuous innovation to provide a smarter, safer, and cleaner driving experience for the next generation. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations and any majority owned subsidiary by 2040, with an interim target of 65% by 2030.

Hyundai Wia is the second largest automotive parts company in Republic of Korea. Hyundai Wia is responding to latest trends in car manufacturing, developing the eco-friendly four-wheel drive system and integrated thermal management solutions for electric and hydrogen vehicles. Additionally, Hyundai Wia is focussing on Robotics and Autonomous, making them a future-smart logistics and solutions provider. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations and any majority owned subsidiaries by 2045, with an interim target of 60% by 2030.
Ichigo is a Japanese sustainable infrastructure company that has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its operations by 2025.

Incheon International Airport is the largest airport in South Korea. It is a World-Class air logistics hub and passenger transport hub in northeast Asia. It has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Infineon Technologies is a top 10 global semiconductor company that offers a wide range of semiconductor solutions for automotive, power or industrial applications. Combining entrepreneurial success with responsible action, at Infineon we make life easier, safer and greener. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2025.

Infosys is an Indian multinational corporation that provides business consulting, information technology, software engineering and outsourcing services. The company aims to source 100% renewable electricity by 2030.

INFRONEER Holdings Inc. is a Japanese holding company founded in 2021 and headquartered in Tokyo. INFRONEER offers infrastructure construction services in planning, proposal, design, construction, operation and maintenance, as well as operating the construction business, road paving, manufacturing and sales of construction machinery. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050, with an interim target of 60% by 2030.

Based in the Netherlands, ING is a global financial institution meeting the needs of a broad customer base comprising individuals, families, small businesses, large corporations, institutions and governments. The bank is committed to powering all of its operations 100% with renewable electricity by 2020.

Intel’s commitment to positive global impact is embedded in its purpose to create world-changing technology that enriches the lives of every person on earth. For more than a decade, Intel has been one of the top voluntary corporate purchasers of green power. Building on the progress of the company’s 2020 goals, Intel has set goals to achieve 100% renewable electricity use across its global manufacturing operations and conserve an additional 4 billion kWh of energy by 2030.

Interface, Inc. is a global modular flooring manufacturer headquartered in the US. The company achieved its goal of sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations in 2016 – four years ahead of schedule.

Headquartered in the US, International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. is a leading innovator of products that consumers taste, smell, or feel in fine fragrances and beauty, detergents and household goods, foods and beverages. The company is committed to procure 100% of its electricity from renewable sources in the shortest practical timescale possible and as financially feasible. It has an interim goal of achieving 75% by 2025.

Investec, headquartered in South Africa,  is a distinctive bank and wealth manager, driven by commitment to their core philosophies and values. The company delivers exceptional service to our clients in the areas of banking and wealth management, striving to create long-term value for all of their stakeholders and contributing meaningfully to people, communities and planet. Investec commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2030.

Iron Mountain Incorporated is a global business dedicated to storing, protecting and managing information and assets. The company joined RE100 with a target to source 100% renewable electricity for its global operations by 2050.

IWG, originating as Regus, was founded by Mark Dixon over 30 years ago when he noticed local business people in Brussels were regularly conducting meetings around the small tables of local coffee shops. Today, IWG has around 3500 locations - covering every continent and time zone across the globe with many well-loved brands including Regus, Spaces, HQ and Signature. The company’s mission is to ensure everyone has a great day at work whilst protecting people and planet. IWG is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

J. Front Retailing Group is a business group with sales of over 1 trillion yen. It is involved in a wide range of businesses in Japan, including shopping centres, trading companies, and the construction and interior design of department stores. J. Front Retailing Group has committed to sourcing 100% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2050.

Japan Real Estate Investment Corporation, or JRE, is a Japanese real estate investment trust that invests in, owns, and leases office properties. The majority of the company’s real estate portfolio sits in Tokyo’s 23 wards, and the Tokyo metropolitan area. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its own operations by 2050, with an interim target of 90% by 2030.

JCDecaux, the number one outdoor advertising company worldwide, reaches more than 410 million people on the planet every day. Sustainable development has been a crucial commitment since its creation, and concrete objectives for this are set across all its entities, covering every operational aspect of its business. Joining RE100 was part of this strategy, and the company committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2022.

JD Sports Fashion Plc is a leading international multichannel retailer of sports, fashion and outdoor brands. JD has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity for its European operations by 2022 with all major global entities to be renewable by 2025.

JK Lakshmi Cement is one of India’s leading manufacturers of grey cement. Over four decades, the company has partnered India's multi-sectoral infrastructure needs on the strength of its product excellence, customer orientation and technology leadership. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2040.

JK Paper Ltd., established in 1962, is a large producer of office paper, coated paper and packaging board. It is headquartered in New Dehli, with 3 facilities in Odisha, Gujarat & Telengana, and the company has a combined capacity of about 7.5 lakh TPA. It exports its products to 35 countries covering USA, Middle East, SAARC and Africa. JK Paper joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

JK Tyre & Industries is a Indian tyre manufacturing company, headquartered in New Delhi. It joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2050.

Johnan Shinkin Bank is a financial institution operating mainly in Tokyo, Japan. Jonan Shinkin Bank has set a target to source 100% renewable electricity by 2050. As the first financial institution to join RE100 in Japan, Jonan Shinkin Bank will focus on advocating for renewable electricity in the financial sector.

Jola International Co., Ltd is a biotech and pharma company that specializes in developing skin care products, including facial masks under their brand, TTM (Timeless Truth Masks). Founded in 2012, their masks are now sold in department stores and pharmacies in 25 countries. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

JSW Cement is India’s leading ‘Green Cement’ manufacturer headquartered in Mumbai. The company has a strong commitment towards innovation in sustainability and technology to offer eco-friendly construction and building solutions to our customers. Its plants utilise the by-products produced by the steel industry to manufacture green cement. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.
Jupiter Asset Management is a leading, London-based investment management company, and achieved its goal of sourcing 100% renewable energy for its electricity use in 2017, one year ahead of schedule.

Kakao Corporation (Kakao) is an information technology service provider headquartered in South Korea. The company provides diverse mobile services, including KakaoTalk, the top South Korean messenger, and operates businesses in areas such as commerce, mobility, pay, games, music, and content. Kakao Corporation commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2040.

Kao Corporation is a consumer goods and chemicals company headquartered in Japan. The company is a leading manufacturer in the fields of hygiene, beauty and health, with its portfolio of over 20 leading brands such as Attack, Bioré, Goldwell, Jergens, John Frieda, Kanebo, Laurier, Merries, and Molton Brown. Kao has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2030.

KB Financial Group is a leading financial services provider offering broad range of financial products and services in South Korea. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

KDDI Group is a Japanese integrated telecommunications company providing a range of services including mobile, fixed-line, and IT services, and with operations in more than 100 countries globally. KDDI commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2050.

Kellanova, a leading maker of breakfast cereals and snacks, joined RE100 with a goal to achieve 100% renewable electricity by 2050, aligned with its commitment to reduce its direct greenhouse gas emissions by 65%. Having made strong progress against this target, Kellanova has since brought its target year forward to 2030.

Kerry is the global leader in the development of taste and nutrition solutions for the food, beverage and pharmaceutical markets. Its broad technology foundation, customer‐centric business model, and industry‐leading integrated solutions capability make Kerry the co‐creation partner of choice. Kerry is also an industry‐leading manufacturer of chilled food products primarily to the Irish and UK markets. Kerry employs over 26,000 people globally, including over 1,000 food scientists, and its global presence extends across 148 manufacturing facilities in 32 countries. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2025.

Keurig Dr Pepper is a leading producer and distributor of hot and cold beverages, with a portfolio of more than 125 iconic brands. The company has a target to use 100% renewable power globally by 2025.

King Yuan Fu Packaging (KYF) is a Taiwanese company that produces food packaging. Their packaging has been sold to companies in over 30 countries and is used by numerous domestic companies. KYF has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.
Kirin Holdings Company is a global beverage & pharmaceuticals company headquartered in Japan. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2040.

The KOKUSAI ELECTRIC Group has a business history for nearly 70 years and was carved out in 2018 as a specialty manufacturer in semiconductor manufacturing equipment. The company's headquarters are in Tokyo, Japan, and it specializes in the indispensable thin film deposition, and treatment in which to improve the thin film quality of the semiconductor device manufacturing process. The KOKUSAI ELECTRIC Group joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2030.

Konica Minolta, Inc. is a Japanese multinational technology company manufacturing office and health care equipment for the public, commercial and corporate business sectors. The company has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.

Korea Water Resources Corporation (K-water) provides world class water resources management services based on innovative technologies and 50 years of comprehensive know-how, in South Korea. As the water supply and dam operation leader in Korea, K-water is committed to reaching 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2050.

KPMG is a global organization of independent professional services firms providing Audit, Tax and Advisory services. The network operates in 146 countries and territories and in FY20 had close to 227,000 people working in member firms around the world. KPMG has committed to source 100 percent renewable electricity by 2022 in Global Board countries, and by 2030 for the wider global organization.

KPN is a Dutch landline and mobile telecommunications company that believes switching to renewable energy consumption is one key way to address climate change. All of KPN's electricity has come from renewable sources of energy since 2011.

Kumagai Gumi Co., Ltd is a Japanese construction company involved in planning, designing and implementing construction work, as well as providing engineering, management and consulting services. They have been involved in numerous large-scale construction projects including the Shinjuku Nomura Building and Taipei101. Kumagai Gumi has set a target to source 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.

Lam Research Corporation, established in 1980, and headquartered in Fremont, California, is a global supplier of wafer fabrication equipment and services to the semiconductor industry. With operations across North America, Asia, and Europe, Lam Research employs approximately 17,450 people. Lam Research has set a goal to source 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

Landsec is the largest commercial property development and investment company in the UK. The company set a goal to source 100% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2019, as part of its ambition to be a sustainability leader, recognising the need to transition to a low carbon economy.

Lear Corporation is a global automotive technology company, headquartered in the USA. With operations in 38 countries around the world, Lear provides seating and E-Systems technology, enabling superior in-vehicle experiences for consumers, and is driven by a commitment to innovation, operational excellence, and sustainability. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

The LEGO Group is one of the world's leading manufacturers of creative play materials, and wants to make a positive impact on the planet and the children who will inherit it. Since 2012 the LEGO Group has invested approximately US$890 million in offshore wind power. In May 2017 the company joined RE100 and achieved its ambition to balance 100% of its electricity use with energy from renewable sources.

Legrand is a global specialist in electrical and digital building infrastructures, headquartered in Limoges, France. With 38,000 employees in 90 countries, it works towards making the buildings of tomorrow more sustainable. Legrand joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2030.

LG Electronics, headquartered in South Korea, is an electronics company focused on developing new innovations across the globe. The company is committed to providing electronic products that help customers live better, and is composed of the business headquarters of H&A (Home Appliance & Air Solution), HE (Home Entertainment), VS (Vehicle component Solutions), and BS (Business Solutions). The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

Link Logistics operates a portfolio of over 400 million square feet of logistics real estate in key markets throughout the United States. The company has set a target to source 100% renewable electricity across its operations by 2024.

LITEON is a provider of optoelectronic semiconductor components and power management modules founded in Taipei, Taiwan in 1975. As an electrical & electronic equipment company, LITEON offers solutions powered by cloud computing, automotive electronics, 5G, and AIoT. LITEON commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across their global operation sites by 2040
Lixil Group Corporation is a Japanese manufacturer of building materials and housing equipment, headquartered in Tokyo. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2050.

Lloyds Banking Group is a leading UK based financial services group providing a wide range of banking, insurance and financial services, focused on personal and commercial customers. The Group has announced that it has sourced 100% renewable electricity globally since January 2019, meeting its 2020 target early. The bank aims to increase the proportion of electricity sourced via additional renewable projects, which would not otherwise be built or become operational without their involvement, to at least 60% by 2025.

Logitech is a leader in designing products that help connect people to digital and cloud experiences. As a multi-brand company, Logitech enables better experiences consuming, sharing and creating digital content for computing, gaming, video and music. Logitech has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

Lotte Wellfood is headquartered in South Korea, and is a leader in the domestic food industry. The company manufactures and sells lifecycle-type brands ranging from infants to middle-aged, including confectionery, bakery, ice cream and oil. The company has rebranded to Lotte Wellfood after the merge of Lotte Confectionary and Lotte Food, to reflect its commitment to wellness. Lotte Wellfood commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2040.

LS ELECTRIC is an international power and automation business, with a specialism in the field of smart energy. It is headquartered in Anyang, South Korea. LS ELECTRIC declared ESG management with the vision of ‘Sustainable Future with Green energy Solution’ for sustainable growth. LS ELECTRIC commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across their entire, global operations by 2040.

LSEG (London Stock Exchange Group) is a diversified global financial markets infrastructure and data business. For over 300 years LSEG has supported financial stability, growth of communities and economies globally enabling businesses and economies around the world to fund innovation, manage risk and create jobs. After acquiring Refinitiv in 2021, LSEG took over the RE100 commitment. LSEG commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2022.

Founded in Vancouver, Canada in 1998, lululemon athletica is a technical athletic apparel company for yoga, running and training, selling its products internationally in its 460 stores and online. lululemon has a target to source 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2021.

LY Corporation is a holdings company with group companies that provide more than 200 services, including Yahoo! JAPAN, one of Japan's largest portal website and LINE, one of Japan's largest communication app. The company commits to globally sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2030, with an interim target of 80% by 2025.

The L’OCCITANE Group markets natural beauty and well-being products throughout the world. Since its creation, the French cosmetics company has placed the protection of nature and the environment at the heart of its business strategy. L’OCCITANE is transitioning to 100% renewable electricity globally by 2025, with an interim goal of 80% by 2020.

M&G plc is a London based investments business, managing investments for both individuals and for large institutional investors, such as pension funds, around the world. M&G has committed to source 100% renewable electricity for its entire global operations by 2020.

Mace is an international consultancy and construction company, which strives to create more sustainable cities and communities. The UK-based company is committed to achieving 100% renewable electricity globally by 2030.

Mahindra Holidays & Resorts India is a part of the Leisure and Hospitality sector of the Mahindra Group, providing holidays primarily through ownership memberships. The company has a target to power 100% of its global operations with renewable electricity by 2050.

MANE is the leading French group and one of the world leaders in the Flavour and Fragrance industry. The family company has been committed to preserving environment and to fighting against climate change for many years. Engaged in a transition plan aligned with a 1.5°C global warming scenario, each new investment in their subsidiaries’ production facilities is inseparable from investments in renewables. MANE is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.
Mars has made a commitment to conduct business in a way that is good for the company, good for people and good for the planet. Mars has a target to source 100% renewable electricity by 2040.

Marui Group is a major player in the Japanese retail and financial sectors with a business model merging retailing and finance that is implemented by its Retailing and FinTech segments. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2030.

Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. The company is a leader in global payments and technology that connects billions of consumers, thousands of financial institutions, and millions of merchants, as well as governments and businesses around the world. Mastercard has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations, beginning in 2020.

McCain Foods is a privately owned family company established in 1957. It is the world's largest manufacturer of frozen potato products and supplies retail and foodservice customers around the globe. It has committed to mitigating its impact on the planet by reducing its carbon footprint and improving operational efficiency. The company committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its French Fry plants by 2030 and across its entire global operation by 2050.

McKinsey & Company is a global management consulting firm that serves leading businesses, governments, non-governmental organisations, and not-for-profits. McKinsey has a target to power 100% of its operations with renewable electricity by 2025 as part of its commitment to be carbon neutral.

Meiji Holdings is a Japanese holding company with subsidiaries that manufacture dairy products, confectionary items, health food and pharmaceuticals. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

Meiji Yasuda, headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, operates primarily in Japan and the United States, offering a wide range of insurance products and services. With more than 52,000 employees, Meiji Yasuda joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Merry is a world-leading manufacturer of electroacoustic products. Headquartered in Taiwan, the company specializes in the design and manufacture of earphones, headsets, speakers, microphones, batteries, and assistive listening devices (ALD) for mobile communication, multimedia and entertainment, accessory, smart homes, medical and healthcare. Merry commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

Mindspace Business Parks REIT, owns office portfolios across India. It has a diversified tenant base numbering over 160 as of March 31, 2021. Most buildings are either Gold or Platinum Green Building Certified (IGBC/LEED). The company has set phase-wise targets to source 35% of total electricity requirements from renewable sources across their entire operations by 2025 and 100% by 2050.

Mirae Asset Securities is a leading financial institution in South Korea that is committed to pioneering a sustainable future through finance with a clear understanding of our environmental roles and responsibilities. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2025.

Mitie is a leading UK facilities management and professional services company which look after a large, diverse, blue-chip customer base, from banks and retailers, to hospitals, schools and critical government strategic assets. The company has joined RE100 having already achieved its 100% renewable electricity target across its entire operations.

Mitsubishi Estate is one of the largest real-estate developers in Japan, involved in office building in the Marunouchi district in central Tokyo, as well as the retail property, residential and hotel businesses. The geographical coverage of the company is global including the US, the UK, mainland Europe, Asia and Oceania. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.

Mitsui Fudosan is a major real estate developer, headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. It operates in leasing, property sales, management, and other services, and has operations across the US, Europe and Asia. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

MORI Building is one of the major Japanese real estate developers headquartered in Minato-ku Tokyo, Japan. It develops and manages towns and buildings in Minato-ku, across Shanghai, and Jakarta. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

Murata Manufacturing Co., Ltd. is a worldwide leader in the design, manufacture and sale of ceramic-based passive electronic components & solutions, communication modules and power supply modules. Murata is committed to the development of advanced electronic materials and leading edge, multi-functional, high-density modules. The company has employees and manufacturing facilities throughout the world. Murata  is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2035.

National Australia Bank serves 9 million customers at more than 900 locations in Australia, New Zealand and around the world. By market share, NAB is Australia’s leading arranger of project finance for renewable energy and was the first Australian bank to become carbon neutral in 2010. NAB has committed to using 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2025.

NatWest is a large international banking and financial services company, serving around 18.9 million customers across the globe. NatWest has committed to achieving 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2025, with an interim target of 90% by 2020.
NAVER Corp. is a global ICT company, providing South Korea’s number one search portal. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

NBN Co Limited designs, operates and provides wholesale broadband services through fibre, wireless and satellite technologies, which comprise Australia’s National Broadband Network. The company commits to purchasing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2026.

NEC Corporation is a Japan-based multinational leader in the integration of IT and network technologies supporting digital solutions that benefit businesses and society. NEC Corporation has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.

Nemera is a world leading partner in drug delivery solutions and combination product services, headquartered in France and with over 2,600 employees in 5 countries. For the company, a forward looking ESG approach is not a nice-to-have but a must-have. Nemera commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.
Nestlé is the world’s largest food and beverage company. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across all 800+ of its global sites by 2025.

New Balance Athletics is an American multinational corporation that designs, manufactures and sells athletic footwear, apparel, and accessories in more than 120 countries worldwide. New Balance has a goal to achieve 100% renewable electricity by 2025, with an interim goal of 75% by 2020.

Next is a UK based retailer offering clothing, footwear and home products with more than 500 stores in the UK and Ireland, as well as around 200 mainly franchised stores in over 30 countries overseas. Next has a target to use 100% renewable electricity globally by 2030 and has already achieved this in the UK and Ireland, which together represent 95% of its total electricity consumption.

NGK INSULATORS is a Japanese manufacturer that uses its unique ceramic technology to continuously create new value required by society in the business fields of energy, ecology, and electronics. NGK is headquartered in Nagoya and is listed on the Nikkei 225, which is an index of the Tokyo Stock Exchange. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Nikon Corporation specializes in products and solutions based on advanced opto-electronics and precision technologies. As well as producing cameras and binoculars, Nikon’s products are used in healthcare, manufacturing and a range of technologies. Nikon has set a target to source 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

Nippon Life Insurance is a group of multiple companies operating life insurance and asset management businesses. Established in 1889, the firm has grown to become one of the largest private asset owners in Japan. Its headquarters are based in Tokyo, while its head office is located in Osaka. It operates life insurance businesses in seven countries outside of Japan, namely the US, Australia, India, China and Thailand, Myanmar, and Indonesia. The company joins RE100 procuring 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2040.

Nishimatsu Construction Co., Ltd is a Japanese construction company whose main areas of business are building construction, civil engineering, real estate development and renewable power generation projects. The company builds tunnels, roads, airports, dams, high-rises, hospitals, hotels, and other civil and commercial projects around the world. Nishimatsu Construction has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2050.

Nitto Denko Co., LTD. is a global supplier of tape, film, and functional material headquartered in Osaka, Japan. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity for its global operations by 2035.

Nokia is a leading technology company that provides solutions across mobile, fixed and cloud networks worldwide. It creates value with intellectual property and long-term research, led by the world-renowned Nokia Bell Labs, by helping to build the capabilities for a more productive, sustainable and inclusive world. Nokia commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2025.

Nomura Real Estate Holdings is a Japanese holding company for real estate business. It owns companies that develop and operate residential houses, office buildings, logistics facilities, retail facilities, hotels, a property management company, real estate brokerage companies and a real estate investment company. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global group operations by 2050.

Nomura Research Institute (NRI) is a leading business consultancy and technology solution firm, headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. NRI has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity globally by 2050, and 70% at data centres by 2030.

Noritz Corporation develops and manufactures water heaters. Headquartered in Japan, they have operations in over 13 countries including America, China and Australia. Noritz Corporation has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.

Novo Nordisk is a global healthcare company with more than 90 years of innovation and leadership in diabetes care, and experience and capabilities to help people defeat other serious chronic conditions: haemophilia, growth disorders and obesity.
