Headquartered in Denmark, Novo Nordisk has set a target to source 100% renewable power at all production sites by 2020.

Novozymes is the world leader in biological solutions. The company works with customers, partners and the global community to improve agricultural and industrial performance while preserving the planet's resources and helping build better lives. Its bioinnovation enables higher agricultural yields, low-temperature washing, energy-efficient production, renewable fuel, and many other benefits. It is the world’s largest provider of enzyme and microbial technologies with global operations across Europe, Asia and the Americas. Novozymes commits to source 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

Nxtra by Airtel is a subsidiary of Bharti Airtel Limited, having its headquarters in Gurugram, India. They offers largest network of secure, scalable, and sustainable data centers to all the leading global enterprises, hyperscalers, SMEs, and government organizations. Being associated with both data center and telecom industry for more than 2 decades, Nxtra by Airtel provides a platform of hyper connected core and edge data centers across 120+ locations. Nxtra is actively adopting and investing innovative green energy technologies to achieve its Net Zero target. Nxtra commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2035.

O'right is a Taiwanese haircare brand. The company is a global leader in sustainable haircare and has won several awards for its products. O'right joined RE100 with a target to source 100% renewable electricity for its operations by 2025.

Okamura Corporation is a Japanese company based in Yokohama that provides high-quality products and services for offices, education, healthcare, R&D, commercial facilities and distribution centres. Okamura commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.
Ono Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. is one of Japan's global pharmaceutical companies. The company commits to source 100% renewable electricity across it’s entire global operations by 2050.

Otsuka Holdings is a holding company of the Otsuka group of companies, whose origins date back to 1921. The Otsuka group aims to contribute to the health of people around the world through two main pillars: the pharmaceutical business for the diagnosis and treatment of diseases and the nutraceutical business to support the maintenance and promotion of everyday health. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050, with 169 subsidiaries globally included in the RE100 commitment.

P&G serves consumers around the world with one of the strongest portfolios of trusted, quality, leadership brands. The P&G community includes operations in approximately 70 countries worldwide. Based in the US, the company set a goal to power its plants with 100% renewable electricity by 2030, 20% by 2020.

Panasonic Holdings Corporation, headquartered in Japan, is a worldwide leader in the development of diverse electronic technologies and customer solutions in consumer electronics, housing, automotive, and B2B. The company has a goal to achieve 100% renewable electricity for its global business operations by 2030.

Parques Reunidos Group is an international operator of regional leisure parks with headquarters in Madrid (Spain). Since the establishment of the company in 1967, its history has spanned over 50 years of continued growth and diversification – it has evolved from being a primarily Spanish business into managing a portfolio of more than 50 parks in 11 countries, with more than 18 million visitors every year. The company joins RE100 procuring 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations and commits to continuing to do so.

Pearson, the world’s largest education company, provides a range of education products and services that help people everywhere aim higher and fulfil their true potential. The company has been 'climate neutral' since 2009, an approach it has renewed and refreshed every subsequent year. As part of that commitment it has a policy to only purchase the electricity it uses in its buildings from renewable sources, something it first achieved in 2012 and has maintained ever since.

PepsiCo is a leading food and beverage company whose products are enjoyed by consumers more than one billion times a day in over 200 countries and territories. Its broad product portfolio – which includes Frito-Lay, Gatorade, Pepsi-Cola, Quaker and Tropicana – has 23 brands that generate more than $1 billion each in estimated annual retail sales. As part of the company’s agenda to help build a more sustainable food system, PepsiCo has set a target to source 100% renewable electricity across its company owned and controlled operations and across its entire global operations, including franchises by 2030.

Pernod Ricard is the No.2 worldwide producer of wines and spirits. The group holds one of the most prestigious and comprehensive brand portfolios in the industry. Pernod Ricard has a target to source 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2025.

Pfizer Inc. is a multinational biopharmaceutical company founded in 1849 and headquartered in New York City. Pfizer discovers, develops and produces medicines and vaccines for cancer, rare disease, inflammation & immunology, cardiovascular & metabolic diseases. Pfizer has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

PNC is one of the largest diversified financial services institutions in the US, offering a wide range of services to its customers, from individuals and small businesses, to corporations and government entities. PNC has a target to use 100% purchased electricity for its global operations by 2025.

Primax is a leading supplier of IT, electronic and consumer solutions. Headquartered in Taipei, Taiwan, Primax operates across Asia, The Americas and Europe. Harnessing the potential of cloud technology and IoT, Primax has successfully developed human-machine interface products with touch, voice control, gesture recognition and wearable functions. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Prime Life Technologies Corporation is a corporate group comprising Panasonic Homes Co., Ltd., Toyota Housing Corporation, Misawa Homes Co., Ltd., Panasonic Construction Engineering Co., Ltd., and Matsumura Gumi Corporation. Under the slogan of “Changing the 'ordinary' of life,” the company is developing housing, construction, and urban development businesses. Prime Life Technologies Corporation commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2040.

Founded in 2017 and headquartered in Singapore, Princeton Digital Group develops and operates over 20 data centers across six key digital economies in Asia Pacific: Singapore, Japan, India, Indonesia, China, and Malaysia. Princeton Digital Group joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity for its global operations by 2030.

Prologis, Inc. is the global leader in logistics real estate with a focus on high-barrier, high-growth markets. At December 31, 2023, the company owned or had investments in, on a wholly owned basis or through co-investment ventures, properties and development projects expected to total ~1.2 billion square feet (115 million square meters) in 19 countries. Prologis leases modern logistics facilities to a diverse base of ~6,700 customers across two major categories: business-to-business and retail/online fulfilment. The company joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewables across its entire global operations by 2040.

Belgian telecommunications company Proximus has already met its goal of sourcing 100% of its electricity from renewable energy – a decision that was taken to support its carbon emission reduction strategy. The switch to renewables is helping the company to work to substantially reduce its carbon emissions.

PVH is one of the largest global apparel companies, owning and marketing brands such as Calvin Klein, Tommy Hilfiger, Van Heusen and Speedo. PVH has a target to power 100% of its operations with renewables by 2030 and 50% by 2025.

QBE is an international general insurance and reinsurance company, listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX) and headquartered in Sydney. The company employs more than 12,000 people in over 31 countries. QBE has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by the end of 2025.

Qisda, established in 1984, is a global technology corporate group with businesses spanning across IT, healthcare, smart solutions, networking and communication. The company is a world-leading provider of LCD monitors and projectors, and owns two large general hospitals and stretches across the business of medical devices and channels. Qisda is also the mother company of BenQ, which provides products and services across lifestyle, business, healthcare and education field. The company commits to 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

QTS is a top provider of compliant and secure hybrid cloud, data center, and managed services, including colocation, hosting, and cloud computing. QTS has a target to power its operations with 100% renewable power by 2025, achieving 50% by 2022.

Rackspace, Inc. is a managed cloud company based in the US. The company has committed to 100% renewable electricity throughout its entire operations and joined RE100 with a target to achieve this by 2026.

Radio Flyer is a US toy company best known for its popular red toy wagon. Since 1917, the family-owned company has created icons of childhood that spark imagination and inspire outdoor, active play, resulting in it becoming a loved brand for kid’s wheels around the world. Radio Flyer has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2020.

Rakuten is a global leader in internet services that empower individuals, communities, businesses and society. Founded in Tokyo in 1997 as an online marketplace, Rakuten has expanded to offer services in e-commerce, fintech, digital content and communications to approximately 1.6 billion members around the world. Rakuten is aiming to transition all Group company operations to run on renewable electricity by 2023.

Ralph Lauren Corporation, headquartered in New York City, is a U.S. fashion company designing, marketing, and distributing premium lifestyle products across four categories: apparel, home, accessories, and fragrance. Ralph Lauren commits to powering 100% of its globally owned and operated offices, distribution centers and stores with renewable electricity by 2025.
Reckitt is a British multinational consumer goods group, which owns 'Powerbrands' such as Durex, Air Wick and Dettol. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2030.

RELX is a global provider of information and analytics for professional and business customers across industries. It operates in four major market segments: Scientific, Technical & Medical; Risk & Business Analytics; Legal; and Exhibitions. The company has set a goal to source 100% renewable electricity by 2020, with an interim target of 70% by 2017.

RICOH Company, Ltd. is a multinational electronics company providing document services, consulting, software and hardware to businesses around the world. It was the first Japanese company to join RE100. Ricoh has a target to source 100% renewable electricity by 2050.

ROHM Co., Ltd is a global electronics manufacturer based in Kyoto Japan. ROHM provides semiconductors & electronic components for a broad range of markets, including automotive, industrial and consumer market via its global development and sales network. ROHM commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050, with an interim goal of 65% by 2030.

Royal Philips is a leading health technology company focused on improving people’s lives through meaningful innovation, from healthy living and prevention, to diagnosis, treatment and home care. Philips is committed to carbon neutrality for its global operations by 2020, which includes purchasing 100% of its electricity from renewable sources.

Samsung Display, Co., Ltd. is an industry leader in providing cutting-edge display solutions around the world. Headquartered in South Korea, the company has diversified its display markets, providing not only smartphones, TVs, laptops, and monitors but also smartwatches, VR, game consoles, and automotive applications. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050, with its Chinese production plant switching to 100% renewable energy in 2020.

Samsung Fire & Marine Insurance is a leading non-life insurance company in Korea, offering a variety of insurance products and total risk solution services including fire, marine, automobile, accident, liability, long-term non-life insurance, and private pension to personal customers and commercial clients in domestic and overseas markets. Samsung Fire & Marine Insurance is striving to deliver excellence to customers and become a top financial institution in the 21st century. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2040.

Samsung Life Insurance is a South Korean multinational insurance company headquartered in Seoul, South Korea, and a subsidiary of the Samsung Group. Founded in 1957, it is the largest insurance company in South Korea and a Fortune Global 500 company. Samsung Life's principal products include life, health insurance and annuities, providing extensive protection to its clients. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2040.

Samsung SDI, headquartered in the Republic of Korea, is a world-leading battery and electronic material manufacturer redefining the worlds of electric vehicles, energy storage systems and IT devices. The company drives transformation and innovation to emerge as a ‘Creative Energy and Materials Solution Leader’ across the fields of e-mobility, energy solutions, as well as semiconductors and displays. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

Sanofi is a global biopharmaceutical company that prevents illness with vaccines and provides innovative treatments to fight pain and ease suffering. Sanofi is driven to improve the health of communities and to find new solutions for patients by combining breakthrough science with advanced technology. Sanofi has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030 with an interim target of 75% by 2025.

SAP is a multinational enterprise software corporation based in Germany that strives to ‘help the world run better and improve people’s lives through use of their services’. The company reached its goal of powering all facilities and data centres by 100% renewable electricity in 2014.

Sapporo Holdings Limited. is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. As a global enterprise, Sapporo Holdings Limited has business operations in Alcoholic Beverages, Food and Soft Drinks, and Real Estate. Sapporo Holdings Limited. commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Schindler elevators, escalators, and moving walks transport more than 1.5 billion people up and down buildings and across transportation hubs every day. The Switzerland-based group commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2025.

Schroders is a global active investment manager, which helps institutions and individuals meet their financial goals and prepare for the future. Schroders is committed to targeting 100% global electricity from renewable sources by 2025.

SECOM is a global company headquartered in Japan. The group provides services in a number of areas including security, fire protection, medical, insurance, geospatial information, BPO and ICT. SECOM has a goal to achieve 100% renewable electricity for its global business operations by 2045.

Epson is a Japanese electronics company and one of the world's leading manufacturers of printers for office, home, commerce and industry, and with a world leading presence in robotics and projectors. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2023.

Sekisui Chemical Group manufactures and provides services in a wide range of fields. These include in the housing sector and in the production of materials and chemical products used in construction. It also produces high-performance plastics and medical products, and delivers medical services. Sekisui Chemical Group is made up of more than 200 companies, employing over 27,000 people with operations in over 20 countries and territories. Sekisui Chemical Group will convert 100% of purchased power to renewable energy by 2030.

Sekisui House is one of Japan's largest housebuilders, and the leading supplier of zero energy houses enabling comfortable and low carbon living. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable power by 2040, with an interim target of 50% by 2030.

ServiceNow makes the world work better for everyone. They provide cloud‑based platform and solutions to digitize and unify organizations so that they can find smarter, faster, better ways to make work flow. ServiceNow join RE100 having reached 100% renewable electricity across their entire operations in 2021.

Seven & i Holdings Co., Ltd. is a Japanese diversified retail group that plans, manages, and operates mainly convenience stores, supermarkets, and department stores. It operates 7-Eleven, the world’s largest convenience store chain. The company has committed to source 100% renewable electricity across its operations by 2050.

Sharp Group Corporation, headquartered in Osaka, Japan, was established in 1912. The company manufactures and sells telecommunications equipment, electrical equipment, electronic application equipment, and electronic components. Sharp Group Corporation commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2030.

Shimadzu Corporation has been committed to contributing to society through science and technology since its inception in 1875. The company produces analytical and measuring instruments, medical systems and  machinery for the advancement of human health, the safety and security of society, and industrial processes. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across it’s entire global operations by 2050.

Shinhan Financial Group is one of Korea's major financial holding companies. Through its financing, it aims to grow the value of customers, corporates and society together, with the mission of 'compassionate finance, your companion for the future'. Its subsidiaries provide a full range of financial services, including banking, securities, life insurance, and investment banking. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2040.

Shiseido is a global beauty company headquartered in Japan. With a portfolio of brands mainly in skincare, makeup, and fragrance, it operates in approximately 120 countries and regions around the world. Shiseido commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

Shree Cement Limited is a prominent player in the cement industry, headquartered in Kolkata, India. It has a manufacturing capacity of 60.4 MTPA and a power generation capacity of 1014.85 MW globally as on 30th April 2024. Shree Cement has integrated units at 6 locations and grinding units at 11 locations across India as well as an  integrated unit in UAE. Shree Cement joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity for all its operations globally by 2050.

Siemens AG is a global technology powerhouse focusing on intelligent infrastructure for buildings and distributed energy systems and on automation and digitalization in the process and manufacturing industries. Siemens was the first global industrial company to publicly commit to a carbon neutrality goal by 2030. As part of Siemens' goal to become net-zero, the company has committed to RE100 sourcing renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

As one of the leading wafer manufacturers, Siltronic is globally oriented and operates production facilities in Asia, Europe and USA. Silicon wafers are the foundation of the semiconductor industry and the basis for chips in all electronic applications - from computers and smartphones to electric cars and wind turbines. The international company is highly customer-oriented and focused on quality, precision, innovation and growth. Siltronic employs around 4,500 people worldwide and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2045.

Simplo Group is a professional R&D and manufacturer of lithium-ion battery modules. The company had passed the Science Based Targets (near-term) and commits to using 100% green renewable energy in all its global sites by 2040.

SK Hynix Inc. is a South Korean memory semiconductor supplier of dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) and flash memory chips. Hynix is the world's second-largest memory chipmaker and the world's 3rd-largest semiconductor company. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

SK ie Technology, headquartered in South Korea, is a global material solution provider that offers lithium-ion battery separators (LiBS), image display units and IT devices. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

SK Inc. is an investment-oriented holding company with a digital technology business. It has ownership in companies across diverse areas such as chemicals, information and communications, semiconductor materials, bio·pharmaceutical, logistics and services. The company discovers and promotes new growth areas to secure SK’s future. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

SK Siltron is South Korea's only producer of semiconductor silicon wafers and one of the top five global wafer manufacturers. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

SK Specialty is a Korea-based company engaged in the manufacture and sale of specialty gases and materials. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

SK Telecom Co., Ltd. is a South Korean wireless telecommunications operator and is the country’s largest wireless carrier. It joins the initiative along with its subsidiary SK Broadband, which is one of the largest broadband Internet access providers in South Korea. They are committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across their entire global operations by 2050.

SKC is a leading Korean company in the high-added value materials focusing on mobility, semiconductors, and eco-friendly materials. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

SKF is a leading global supplier of solutions around the rotating shaft including bearings, seals, lubrication, condition monitoring and maintenance services. SKF is represented in more than 130 countries and strives to provide reliable rotation by reducing friction, make things run faster, longer and more sustainable. The SKF Group is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

Sky is Europe's leading entertainment company, serving 21 million customers across Italy, Germany, Austria, the UK and Ireland. Sky offers a broad range of content and innovative new technology designed to give customers a better TV experience. Sky already sources most of its electricity from renewable energy, and aims to achieve 100% renewable electricity by 2020.

Slaughter and May is a multinational law firm, headquartered in London, delivering legal advice to a range of clients, including leading corporations, financial institutions and governments. The company has a target to use 100% renewable electricity globally by 2040.

Sodexo is a French food services and facilities management company headquartered Paris with a presence in 64 countries. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across their entire global operations (scopes 1 & 2) by 2025.

SoftBank Corp. is a leading telecommunications service and solutions provider headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. SoftBank Corp. has committed to procure 100% renewable energy across its operations by 2040 and has set a goal of procuring 60% by 2030

Sony Group Corporation is a leading manufacturer of audio, video, imaging, game, mobile and key devices for the consumer and professional markets. With a diverse business portfolio spanning music, pictures and interactive entertainment to customers worldwide, Sony has set a goal to achieve 100% renewable electricity for its global operations by 2030, with an interim goal of 35% by 2025.

Standard Chartered offers international banking services, working across 59 of the world's most dynamic markets including Asia, Africa and the Middle East. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2025.

Sumitomo Forestry Group is a leading custom-built wooden house company in Japan, that has growing visibility in the housing market in the US and Australia. The company engages in a broad range of businesses globally from forestry management, housing construction to biomass. It has set a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity for its entire global operations by 2040.

Founded in 1909, Sumitomo Rubber Group is a global tire and rubber company based in Japan. Sumitomo Rubber Group has produced many world-first and Japan-first technologies and products, such as 100% petroleum-free natural resource tires, and own the DUNLOP and FALKEN brands. The company is currently expanding its sports and industrial products business, and set "Through innovation we will create a future of joy and well-being for all" as its purpose in their new corporate philosophy framework. The company commits to 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050, with an interim target of 65% by 2030.

Suncorp Group Limited is a leading financial services provider in Australia and New Zealand, offering banking, wealth management and insurance products and services through their brands including Suncorp, AAMI, GIO, Apia, Shannons and Vero. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operation by 2025.

Symrise AG, headquartered in Germany, is a major producer of flavours, fragrances, functional ingredients for foodstuffs as well as basic cosmetics and active ingredients. The company has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2025.

T&D Insurance Group is a life insurance group made up of three core life insurance companies, Taiyo Life Insurance Company, Daido Life Insurance Company, and T&D Financial Life Insurance Company. The group commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

Taiwan Mobile is a leading Taiwanese telecom operator providing mobile, fixed-line, cable TV and broadband services. With an overarching principle of “Think Sustainable, Act Responsible”, the company prides itself on its sustainability objectives and seeks industry cooperation to fulfil these. Taiwan Mobile has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2040.

Takashimaya is a Japanese company that operates a well-known chain of department stores across Asia offering a wide array of products ranging from apparel to foods and homewares. Takashimaya has committed to transition its global operations to 100% renewable electricity by 2050.

Target Corporation is one of the largest merchandise retailers in the US, with over 1,800 stores across the country and employing over 360,000 people worldwide. Target has a goal to source 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030, with an interim goal to achieve 60% by 2025.

Tata Motors Limited is India’s largest automobile manufacturer, producing a range of commercial and passenger vehicles as well as defence and homeland security vehicles. The company has set itself the goal of using 100% renewable electricity across all its own operations, by 2030.

Headquartered in London and founded in 1859, Tate & Lyle has over 3000 employees, operates in 39 countries and is a global supplier of ingredients for food and beverages. Tate & Lyle commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030

TCI Co., Ltd is a Taiwanese manufacturer of dietary supplements, specialist drinks, and skin care products. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2030, with an interim goal of 15-20% by 2020.

TDK is a comprehensive electronic components manufacturer leading the world in magnetic technology. Headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, TDK's diverse range of electronic components are at work all the time inside the familiar products that you use every day. TDK has committed to 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050, an interim target of 50% by 2025 with a commitment to review the ambition of its target date within the first year of membership.

Telefonica S.A. is a global telecommunications provider with 127,000 professionals facilitating communication for 350 million customers in 17 countries worldwide. Head-quartered in Spain, the company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity globally by 2030, with an interim target of 50% by 2020.

Tesco is one of the world’s largest retailers with 6,500 stores in 8 countries. Its science-based climate change targets (own operations) are modelled on a 1.5 degree trajectory, requiring continuing efficiency improvements and renewable electricity. The company is committed to sourcing 100% of its electricity from renewable sources by 2030 – to include over 50% from power purchase agreements and on-site generation. It has an interim target to source 65% renewable electricity by 2020.

The Estée Lauder Companies is one of the world’s leading manufacturers and marketers of quality skin care, makeup, fragrance and hair care products with a diverse portfolio of brands sold in 150 countries. The company has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity globally by 2020.

TK Elevator is an elevator company, becoming independent from thyssenkrupp AG in August 2020. Headquartered in Düsseldorf, Germany, the company’s most important business line is the service business, providing commodity elevators for residential and commercial buildings to cutting-edge, highly customized solutions for state-of-the-art skyscrapers. In addition, its business also consists of escalators and moving walks, passenger boarding bridges, stair and platform lifts. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity in its own operations by 2030.

Toda Corporation is a construction company headquartered in Japan and operating through business in building construction, civil engineering, real estate and renewable power generation projects. The company has a goal to use 100% renewable electricity by 2050.

Tokyo Tatemono is a Japanese real estate company that develops, sells and manages commercial buildings, housing and facilities, and has the longest history of business operations in Japan as a comprehensive real estate company. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2050.

Tokyu Construction Co., Ltd. is a Japanese general contractor that builds commercial, institutional, and residential buildings and also performs civil engineering works for bridges, roads, and transportation systems. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.

Tokyu Corporation is a major private railway company, land developer, hotel and retail store operator in the Greater Tokyo Area of Japan. It has set a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity by 2050.

Tokyu Land Corporation is a real estate company that operates in urban and residential development, wellness, and other businesses. The company has a target to be powered by 100% renewable electricity by 2025.

TOTO is the manufacturer of bathroom fixtures and fittings with business activities around the world, founded in 1917 in Japan. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

TAIWAN PRINTED CIRCUIT BOARD TECHVEST CO., LTD. is a manufacturer of printed circuit boards in Taiwan, founded in 1998. Its products are used in various applications, including LCDs (such as TVs, monitors, and public information displays), information technology and RAM-related products (such as motherboards, solid-state drives, and batteries), as well as automotive and industrial applications. TAIWAN PRINTED CIRCUIT BOARD TECHVEST CO., LTD. joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Trane Technologies is a global climate innovator. Through its strategic brands Trane and Thermo King, and its portfolio of environmental products and services, Trane Technologies bring sustainable, efficient climate solutions to buildings, homes and transportation. Trane Technologies is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity by 2040.

Treasury Wine Estates (TWE) is one of the world’s largest premium wine companies, listed on the Australian Securities Exchange. TWE is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2024.

TRIDL is a facial mask and skincare company operating in Asia and North America, and selling its products worldwide. The company has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity for its global operations by 2048, with an interim target of 30% by 2020.

TSMC is a multinational semiconductor manufacturer headquartered in Taiwan. It has been the world’s largest dedicated semiconductor foundry ever since it was founded in 1987. It provides industry leading process technologies and design enablement solutions to unleash innovation for the global semiconductor industry. With operations spanning Asia, Europe, and North America, TSMC has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

UltraTech Cement Limited is the largest manufacturer of grey cement, white cement and ready-mix concrete in India. It is the first company in India and the second company in Asia to issue dollar-based sustainability linked bonds. UltraTech Cement commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2050.

UMC is a leading global pure semiconductor foundry headquartered in Taiwan. It provides high quality IC production, focusing on logic and specialty technologies, serving every major sector of the electronics industry. It operates semiconductor manufacturing fabs in Taiwan, Japan, China and Singapore. UMC commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2050.

Umicore is the circular materials technology Group. Its activities -- Catalysis, Energy & Surface Technologies and Recycling -- aim to accelerate the global transformation of mobility, respond to the growing need for advanced materials, and contribute to the pursuit of a global circular economy. Umicore’s overriding goal of sustainable value creation is based on the ambition to develop, produce and recycle materials in a way that fulfils its mission: materials for a better life. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2040.

Under Armour is an American sports equipment company that manufactures footwear, sports and casual apparel. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

Unicharm is a global company that operates in approximately 80 countries and regions, with its headquarters located in Tokyo, Japan. Its product lineup includes hygiene products, pet care products, and paper products, among others. For instance, in the hygiene products category, Unicharm offers items such as diapers and menstrual products that support personal health and comfort. Unicharm commits to sourcing 100% renewable  electricity across its entire, global operations by 2030.

Unite Students is the UK’s largest owner, manager and developer of purpose-built student accommodation, providing homes for 74,000 students in 173 properties across 25 cities. Unite Students commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2030 as part of its target to be net zero carbon.

Finnish company Vaisala is a global leader in environmental and industrial measurement, providing a comprehensive range of innovative observation and measurement products and services for chosen weather-related and industrial markets. Vaisala has a goal to become powered by 100% renewable electricity by 2020.

The VELUX Group is a world leading roof window manufacturer. Its products include roof windows, modular skylights, decorative blinds, sun screening products and roller shutters, as well as installation and smart home solutions. The VELUX Group has sales and manufacturing operations in more than 40 countries and around 11,500 employees worldwide. The VELUX Group is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2023.

Verizon Communications was formed in 2000 and is one of the world’s leading providers of technology and communications services. Headquartered in New York City and with a presence around the world, the company offers data, video and voice services and solutions on its networks and platforms, delivering on customers’ demand for mobility, reliable network connectivity and security. Verizon is dedicated to bringing economic, environmental and social change to the communities it serves and has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

VF Corporation is one of the world’s largest apparel, footwear and accessories companies connecting people to the lifestyles, activities and experiences they cherish most through a family of iconic outdoor, active and workwear brands. VF Corporation brands include The North Face, Timberland and Vans, among others.

Virgin Media connects millions of customers to TV, broadband, mobile and telephony services. As one of the UK’s leading internet service providers, Virgin Media has a huge reach, with more than 650 sites, over 12,000 employees, millions of customers, and a network which passes more than 15 million homes and 45,000 businesses. The company sourced all its contracted electricity from renewable sources by end of 2018 and will look to cover off the remaining 0.5% uncontracted in 2020.

Visa is the world’s leader in digital payments. Visa is committed to minimizing the environmental footprint of its payment ecosystem, including through its commitment to source 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by the end of 2019.

Vodafone Group is one of the world’s largest telecommunications companies and provides a range of services including voice, messaging, data and fixed communications. Vodafone joined RE100 with a target to source 100% renewable electricity for all its global operations by 2025.

Voya Financial helps Americans plan, invest and protect their savings to get ready to retire better. The company is committed to conducting business in a way that is environmentally and economically responsible, and as part of these efforts, Voya has purchased clean, emission-free wind energy credits equal to or more than 100% of its electricity usage since 2007.

Walmart Inc. (NYSE: WMT) is a global retailer operating more than 10,500 stores and numerous eCommerce websites under 46 banners in 24 countries. Walmart continues to be a leader in sustainability, corporate philanthropy and employment opportunity. As part of RE100, Walmart is committed to sourcing 100% of its electricity from renewable energy by 2035.

Watami Co., Ltd. is a Japanese restaurant chain, operating 472 domestic food and beverage outlets, and delivering over 230,000 meals daily through 516 branch offices. Group companies also include Watami Farm Corporation, which promotes organic farming through the operation of 12 farms, and Watami Farm & Energy Co., Ltd., which sells power generated through wind and solar power generation. Watami Co., Ltd. has a goal of achieving 100% renewable electricity by 2040, 50% by 2035.

Willmott Dixon is a privately-owned, British contracting company.  Its purpose is to deliver brilliant buildings, transform lives, strengthen communities and enhance the environment so our world is fit for future generations. Willmott Dixon have committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its operations by 2030.

WNC Corporation is a leading network communications solutions provider, headquartered in Taiwan but with additional operations sites in Vietnam and China. WNC provides whole-range communications solutions covering 5G edge infrastructure, satellite broadband, satellite broadcasting, enterprise networking, Wi-Fi devices, automotive connectivity, and smart home. The company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2040.

The Wonderful Company is a privately held US$4 billion global company dedicated to harvesting health and happiness around the world. Its iconic brands include FIJI® Water, POM Wonderful®, Wonderful® Pistachios & Wonderful® Almonds, Wonderful® Halos®, Teleflora®, JUSTIN® Wines, JNSQ® Wines and Landmark® Wines. The company has a target to achieve 100% renewable electricity for its US operations by 2025 and global operations by 2040.

Woolworths Group is Australia and New Zealand's largest retailer, made up of a diverse collection of fresh food, drinks and everyday needs retail businesses. Woolworths Group serves more than 29 million customers across its retail outlets and e-commerce banners every week. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire operations by 2025.

Workday is a leading provider of enterprise cloud applications for finance and human resources, helping customers adapt and thrive in a changing world. The company has purchased an amount of renewable electricity that is equivalent to 100% of its global consumption across offices and data centres since 2008, and now includes both on-site and off-site generation.

WPP is a creative transformation company with a global network of more than 107,000 people in 112 countries. It is a world leader in communications, experience, commerce and technology, and home to many agency networks. WPP’s purpose is to build better futures for its people, clients and communities, and as part of this, has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2025.

company_name: Yanfeng Plastic Omnium Automotive Exterior Systems Co., Ltd.
description: Yanfeng Plastic Omnium Automotive Exterior Systems Co, Ltd. (YFPO), a Sino-French joint venture, is a leading enterprise in the automotive exterior system industry, provides automotive exterior components and relative services in the China market, and currently ranked 1st in market share. Established in 2007 with its headquarters based in Shanghai, China, it currently has nearly 30 sites with over 5000 employees. The company joins RE100 and commits to sourcing renewables across its entire global operations by 2030.

YCH Group is Singapore’s largest home-grown supply chain solutions company. As a regional supply chain management partner to many of the world’s leading brands across the Asia Pacific, the company is committed to sourcing 100% renewable energy across its entire operations by 2030.

Founded in 1924 and based in Taiwan, YFY is a leading paper manufacturer but has also transformed into a multi-industry corporation since it restructured to become an investment management holding company in 2012. YFY pursues strategic investments and spearheads the incubation of forward-looking industries, such as high-tech, biotechnology, and energy. In line with its business model, YFY embraces the principles of a circular economy, as set forth by its second generation founder S.C. Ho. The company endeavours to realise its vision of a low-carbon and low-energy future, harnessing the Group's resources to ensure both environmental preservation and community development, all while generating profit. YFY commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire, global operations by 2040.

Zalando is a leading online platform for fashion and lifestyle. Founded in Berlin in 2008, the company brings fashion to around 32 million active customers in 17 markets, offering clothing, footwear, accessories and beauty. As a key European fashion tech company, Zalando works hard to find digital solutions for every aspect of the fashion journey. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2025.

COSMX was established in 2007 and headquartered in Zhuhai. COSMX is one of the worldwide major suppliers of consumer Li-ion batteries, and has long served well-known customers in the field of PCs, notebooks, smart phones and other fields.In the field of power batteries, COSMX have is a supplier for many automotive manufacturers after years of accumulation and recently entered the fields of electric motorcycles, automotive start-up batteries. The company joins RE100 and commits to sourcing 100% renewables across its entire operations by 2050.

Zoetis is the leading  animal health company and discovers, develops, manufactures and commercializes medicines, vaccines and diagnostic products for livestock and pets. The company has committed to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its global operations by 2030.

LOTTE CHILSUNG BEVERAGE is a leading beverage company based in Korea which manufactures beverage and liquor and exports to overseas markets worldwide. The company commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2040.

Gokin Solar is a renewable equipment manufacturing company, headquartered in Zhuhai, China. Founded in 2019 and with over 6,000 global employees. It shipped over 150GW of PV wafers from 2021 to 2024, while its cumulative PV module shipments have reached 7GW.The company joins RE100 as a gold member and commits to sourcing 100%renewable electricity across its global operational bases by 2030.

Ming Yang Smart Energy is a global leader in renewable energy equipment and smart energy solutions. Founded in 1993 and headquartered in Zhongshan, China, the company is privately owned and listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange (601615.SH) and the London Stock Exchange (MYSE). Today, more than 25,000 employees worldwide support our mission to accelerate the energy transition.  Ming Yang is one of the world’s largest wind turbine manufacturers, with a proven track record in onshore and offshore wind and an expanding portfolio across solar, hydrogen, storage, and integrated smart energy systems. The company joins RE100 and commits to source 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2035.

The Suzlon Group is a leading global renewable energy solutions provider, with approximately 21.1 GW of wind energy capacity installed across 17 countries. Headquartered at Suzlon One Earth in Pune, India, the Group includes Suzlon Energy Limited (NSE: SUZLON, BSE: 532667) and its subsidiaries. A vertically integrated organisation, Suzlon has in-house R&D centres in Germany, the Netherlands, Denmark, and India, and manufacturing facilities across India. Operating for over 30 years and with a diverse workforce of ~7,800 employees, Suzlon manages 15.1 GW of assets and an additional ~6 GW installed outside India. Its portfolio includes the advanced 2.x MW and 3.x MW series of wind turbines. The company joins RE100 as a gold member and commits to sourcing 100% renewable electricity across its entire global operations by 2030.
